data.title: Arqit and Sparkle achieve quantum-resistant encryption
data.description:
• Arqit Quantum (ARQQ) and Sparkle announced on Wednesday that they have successfully demonstrated quantum-resilient data transmission across Sparkle’s optical network in Greece.
  • The company said that the proof of concept (POC), implemented over Sparkle metropolitan optical ring in Athens, shows that Arqit’s encryption technology can be embedded directly into the optical transport layer, protecting high-capacity networks against future quantum threats without impacting performance.
  • Sparkle validated that sensitive data can be secured at the physical network layer without compromising performance.
  • Following the deployment of Arqit’s technology, end-to-end encryption was successfully applied to multi-100G optical links, Sparkle added. This demonstrated that quantum-resistant protection can be delivered at the speed of light, ensuring ultra-secure data transmission without compromising performance.
  • “Securing the world’s data networks against emerging threats requires solutions that are both effective and practical,” said Andy Leaver, CEO of Arqit. “Telecom operators are looking for proven, low-disruption paths to quantum-safe security, and this POC demonstrates a ready-now path. It shows that quantum-safe encryption can be embedded directly into existing optical infrastructure, protecting high-capacity data links without disrupting performance or operations.”
  • ARQQ +2.13% premarket to $28.17.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: QuinStreet acquires digital marketplace platform HomeBuddy for $190M
data.description:
• QuinStreet (NASDAQ:QNST) stated on Wednesday that it had entered into a definitive agreement to acquire SIREN GROUP d/b/a HomeBuddy on November 30, 2025.
  • The acquisition will extend the modernized home services platform by adding an important new product line, a consumer brand with important digital media along with new networks, technology, and integrations, and new home services professionals to the company's network.
  • The company will acquire HomeBuddy for $115M in cash at closing and $75M in post-closing payments payable equally over four years.
  • The transaction is expected to close in early 2026.
  • HomeBuddy generated approximately $141M in revenue for the year ended September 30, 2025.

data.title: Microsoft slides amid report it's cutting software sales quotas tied to AI
data.description:
• Microsoft (MSFT) shares slid 2% in premarket trading on Wednesday after The Information reported that the tech giant is cutting software sales quotas tied to artificial intelligence.
  • The Windows maker has lowered its forecast for how fast customers will be able to spend money on AI agents, after a number of salespeople missed their goals, the news outlet added, citing people familiar with the matter. The quotas have been missed across multiple divisions, two salespeople in Microsoft's Azure unit told the news outlet.
  • As such, Microsoft is working with its salespeople and customers to deal with their resistance on how much extra money they pay for AI.
  • Microsoft did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: Hims & Hers to buy YourBio Health for blood sampling technology
data.description:
Hims & Hers Health (HIMS) has entered into an agreement to acquire YourBio Health, a Boston-based maker of capillary whole blood sampling technology.

Financial terms weren’t disclosed. The all-cash transaction is expected to close in early 2026 and will be funded from Hims & Hers’ balance sheet.

The acquisition combines Hims & Hers’ wellness platform with YourBio’s patented, virtually painless micro-needle technology to modernize the overall blood collection experience.

YourBio Health’s TAP device, built on HALO technology, uses bladeless microneedles thinner than an eyelash to produce capillary blood samples in seconds, without the pain or complexity of traditional methods.

“The next generation of health innovation will be built on proprietary technologies that make wellness simpler, more comfortable, and more human,” said Andrew Dudum, founder and CEO of Hims & Hers. “YourBio’s patented TAP and HALO technologies represent major advancements in user experience and design, and we’re excited to bring them into the Hims & Hers ecosystem.”

data.title: Wells Fargo initiates Oracle at Overweight as it holds nearly $500B in AI deals
data.description:
Wells Fargo initiated coverage of Oracle (ORCL) with an Overweight rating due in part to its nearly $500B in artificial intelligence deals with companies such as OpenAI (OPENAI), xAI (X.AI), Meta Platforms (META) and TikTok (BDNCE).

"We see ORCL set to reach a similar scale to the next closest hyperscaler by 2029, underpinned by success landing numerous major AI infra projects (most notably OpenAI's 4.5GW / $300B+ compute contract)," said Wells Fargo analysts, led by Michael Turrin, in a Wednesday investor note. "We estimate OCI will reach ~16% of the cloud market by 2029, up sharply from just 5% in 2025, though still trailing Amazon (AMZN)/Microsoft (MSFT) at 30%. Beyond '29, expect gap further narrows as OCI proves able to handle complex AI infra across range of hardware types."

Wells Fargo also set a $280 price target on the stock.

Besides Oracle's growing position in the cloud infrastructure market, its high-margin software business should also benefit from cloud growth.

"Across its ~$40B software portfolio, ORCL remains the outright leader in data (database + data platform) and a key player in business/industry apps (Fusion, healthcare, etc.)—a leader in more than a dozen Gartner MQs," Turrin said. "We expect ORCL's newfound leadership in AIaaS likely drives flywheel of adoption toward app layer as customers consolidate spend (and vice versa from apps to infra)—a similar playbook used by peer MSFT."

However, there is an element of risk related to Oracle's partnership with OpenAI and possible shrinking gross margins. Oracle's debt burden could also increase due to the massive infrastructure buildout.

"OpenAI represents a significant part of its committed base (~60% pro forma RPO), while well-funded, changes in demand for OpenAI could pose risks," Turrin added.  "GPU-based compute shift brings lower GMs (30-40%) lowering corporate GM profile (80% FY22 to <50% FY29) & OM profile."

data.title: Delta sees ~$200M pre-tax profit impact in Q4 due to govt shutdown - filing
data.description:
Ahead of its presentation at the Morgan Stanley Conference, Delta Air Lines (DAL) disclosed in a Securities and Exchange filing that due to the government shutdown in November, its fourth-quarter pre-tax profit will be impacted by about $200M. 

The impact translates to a hit of 25 cents on its bottom line, Delta said in the filing on Wednesday.

The airline also said growth in travel bookings is back to its initial expectations, demand remains healthy for the remainder of the holiday period, and trends are looking strong for early 2026.

In November, CEO Ed Bastian had warned that the shutdown would cost the company a "significant amount" after a large number of flights were cancelled due to the government mandated flight reductions.

Shares of the company are marginally up in premarket trading. DAL on average has a "buy" rating from SA analysts and a "strong buy" from Wall Street and Seeking Alpha Quant.

Stock is up 7.7% so far this year, below the benchmark S&P index's 16% gain and the S&P 500 Passenger Airlines index's 21.3% climb for the same period.

data.title: Monro appoints Peter Fitzsimmons as CEO
data.description:
• Monro (MNRO) announced on Wednesday that it has entered into a formal employment agreement directly with Peter Fitzsimmons to continue as the company’s full-time president and chief executive officer, effective immediately.
  • The company also said that Fitzsimmons will also join the company’s board of directors and executive committee, effective immediately.
  • Fitzsimmons previously served as the president and chief executive officer of the company pursuant to an engagement letter between the company and AP Services, an affiliate of AlixPartners. 
  • In connection with this announcement, Fitzsimmons has resigned from AlixPartner and its affiliate AP Services, LLC, effective December 2, 2025.
  • MNRO +0.10% premarket to $18.93.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Enphase starts shipments of IQ EV Charger 2 in U.S.
data.description:
Enphase Energy (ENPH) +0.8% pre-market Wednesday after announcing the first production shipments of its next-generation IQ EV Charher 2 to customers across the U.S.

The IQ EV Charger 2 "uses solar-aware charging that captures surplus solar power and adjusts charging in 1-amp steps to maximize clean energy use," the company said, adding that a single SKU supporting up to a 60-amp breaker simplifies inventory for installers, RFID access control keeps charging secure, and load balancing helps avoid panel upgrades by matching power to available capacity.

"EV charging is becoming a natural extension of the home energy system, and the IQ EV Charger 2 gives homeowners a smarter, faster, and more beautiful way to charge with solar," Enphase (ENPH) Senior VP Jayant Somani said.

In addition to the first U.S. shipments, the IQ EV Charger 2 is now also available in Canada, following its debut earlier this year across Europe, Australia, and New Zealand, with additional global availability planned for 2026, the company said.

data.title: Intercontinental Exchange’s November average daily volume up 5% Y/Y
data.description:
• Intercontinental Exchange (ICE), which operates the New York Stock Exchange, reported 5% Y/Y growth in total average daily volume ((ADV)) for November.
  • Open interest rose 14% Y/Y, including a record interest of 112.9M lots on November 21.
  • Total Energy ADV grew 7% Y/Y, with oil ADV up 12% Y/Y. Total Financials ADV, meanwhile, rose 4% Y/Y, with open interest up 33% Y/Y, including record futures of 15.7M lots on November 21.

data.title: DiamondRock Hospitality declares $0.08 dividend
data.description:
• DiamondRock Hospitality (DRH) declares $0.08/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • The company also declared stub dividend of $0.04/share, bringing total dividend to $0.36/share in 2025, up 12.5% from prior dividend of $0.32 in 2024.
  • Forward yield 3.59%
  • Payable Jan. 14; for shareholders of record Dec. 31; ex-div Dec. 31.
  • See DRH Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.
